Draft Incident Report for SOC Analyst Review

Document Control
Report Title: Draft Incident Report for SOC Analyst Review
Incident ID: INC-WANNACRY-TEST
Alert ID: NW-WCRY-445-0001
Case Title: WannaCry Ransomware Activity - mssecsvc.exe
Report Version: 1.0
Report Status: Generated for analyst review
Validation Status: Requires analyst validation
Report Completeness Score: 65/100
Report Completeness Status: Completeness needs review
Report Classification: Internal / Restricted
Prepared By: Reporting Agent
Reviewed By: SOC Analyst, pending
Report Generation Approved By: Soong Yang
Created At: 2026-07-03T09:22:16.235255+00:00
Source System: RSA NetWitness SIEM
Generation Mode: deterministic_facts_plus_template_export
RAG Used: True
RAG Status: Local template export context built

1. Executive Summary

1.1 Incident Overview
This incident is assessed as a Critical-severity, High-confidence Likely True Positive involving WannaCry ransomware activity. The known affected scope is FINANCE-WKS-017 (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation criticality, Endpoint) and ACME\lim.huiwen (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation, MFA: Pending analyst validation). The case is supported by EV-001: severity: Critical. EV-002: risk_score: 98.0. EV-003: host: FINANCE-WKS-017. EV-004: ip: 10.20.14.117. EV-005: username: ACME\\lim.huiwen. Malicious or suspicious indicators include No malicious or suspicious IOCs provided. Containment is currently not_contained. Report generation approval status: Approved by Soong Yang. Containment approval status: Pending Analyst Approval. Recommended containment action: Isolate FINANCE-WKS-017 from the network pending SOC Analyst Approval. Containment execution status: Not Contained. Final analyst review status: Requires Final Analyst Review. Data impact assessment: No evidence of data access, data exfiltration, encryption impact, or personal data exposure was provided in the current telemetry. These items remain unresolved and require analyst validation before closure or notification decisions.
- Data access: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Data exfiltration: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Encryption / modification / deletion: Not confirmed by supplied host telemetry
- Personal data involvement: Cannot determine from current evidence
- Notification requirement: Legal / Compliance decision only if data impact is confirmed
- Business impact context: Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence.
- Security risk context: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps. The report is not finalised because these validation items remain open: Medium: No additional host telemetry was supplied.

1.2 Executive Conclusion
Based on the available evidence, this incident is assessed as Critical severity with High confidence. The current classification is Likely True Positive, and the most likely scenario is WannaCry ransomware activity.
Incident Status: Generated for analyst review
Current Lifecycle Stage: Reporting and analyst review
Containment Status: not_contained
Report Generation Approval Status: approved
Report Generation Approved By: Soong Yang
Containment Approval Status: Pending analyst approval
Containment Execution Status: not contained
Final Analyst Review Status: Requires final analyst review
Final Disposition: Pending SOC analyst review

1.2.1 Correlated Alerts in This Incident
No additional confirmed correlated alerts were attached to this incident ticket at report generation time.

1.2.2 PowerShell Encoded Command Analysis
No decoded PowerShell command analysis was available for this incident. If this was a PowerShell Encoded Command alert, investigation confidence is limited by missing command-line telemetry.

1.3 Business Impact Summary
Known Business Impact: Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence.
Security Risk: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps.
Unconfirmed Impact: Further compromise, spread, persistence, and data exposure remain subject to analyst validation.
Affected Assets Count: 1
Affected Users Count: 1
IOC Count: 9
Evidence Item Count: 16
Timeline Event Count: 5

1.4 Key Findings
Finding ID | Finding | Status | Confidence | Evidence References | Interpretation
KF-001 | The incident classification is Likely True Positive. | Fact | High | investigation_result.json | Classification is taken from investigation output first, with triage as fallback.
KF-002 | Affected scope has available context. | Fact | High | EV-003, EV-004 | Assets and users are derived only from supplied ticket, alert, triage, and investigation context.
KF-003 | Approval status is approved. | Fact | High | approval_result.json | Approval data is recorded only from analyst approval context.

2. Basis of Review

2.1 Purpose of Report
This report provides a structured post-incident review of incident INC-WANNACRY-TEST. It is intended to support SOC analyst review, management decision-making, technical remediation, control improvement, and future response readiness.

2.2 Review Scope
This review covers the available evidence relating to alert detection, entity extraction, alert normalisation, threat intelligence enrichment, triage assessment, investigation findings, containment and approval status, impact assessment, lessons learnt, and remediation planning.

2.3 Out of Scope
The following areas are outside the scope of this report unless separately authorised: full legal investigation, full forensic disk imaging, malware reverse engineering, regulatory breach determination, disciplinary investigation, third-party contractual liability assessment, and complete enterprise-wide compromise assessment.

2.4 Evidence Basis
This report was prepared using the evidence available at generation time, including SIEM alerts, endpoint telemetry, network logs, authentication logs, threat intelligence enrichment, analyst notes, approval records, and local policies, procedures, or playbooks.
Findings may be updated if additional logs, forensic artefacts, stakeholder inputs, or external intelligence become available.

2.5 Review Limitations
Log Availability: Dependent on submitted input files and available SOC telemetry.
Endpoint Visibility: Dependent on endpoint evidence included in investigation_result.json.
Network Visibility: Dependent on available SIEM, NetWitness, proxy, DNS, and firewall logs.
Threat Intelligence Coverage: Threat intelligence reputation may be incomplete and must be interpreted with local telemetry.
Timestamp Accuracy: Timeline accuracy depends on source clock synchronisation.
Evidence Completeness: Missing evidence is documented as evidence gaps for analyst follow-up.

3. Severity, Confidence and Evidence Methodology

3.1 Severity Rating Methodology
Critical: Confirmed or highly likely compromise affecting critical systems, sensitive data, essential services, or requiring urgent containment.
High: Strong evidence of malicious activity with meaningful risk to systems, users, data, or operations.
Medium: Suspicious activity requiring investigation, but limited evidence of compromise or business impact.
Low: Weak indicators, limited exposure, minimal impact, or activity likely to be benign.
Informational: Not an incident, but useful for awareness, monitoring, or tuning.

3.2 Applied Severity Assessment
Severity: Critical
Severity Reason: No explicit severity rationale was supplied; validate against triage and investigation evidence.
Confidence: High
Confidence Reason: No explicit confidence rationale was supplied; validate against evidence completeness and source telemetry.
Overall Report Confidence: High

3.3 Confidence Rating Methodology
High: Multiple reliable evidence sources support the finding, with minimal conflicting information.
Medium: Some evidence supports the finding, but there are evidence gaps or unresolved questions.
Low: Limited evidence, incomplete logs, uncertain threat intelligence correlation, or unverified assumptions.

3.4 Evidence Reliability Model
Evidence Type | Reliability | Notes
Raw SIEM Logs | High | Strong evidence if source integrity, timestamp accuracy, and log completeness are reliable.
Endpoint Telemetry | High | Strong evidence for process, file, registry, user, and host-level activity.
Network Logs | High | Strong evidence for traffic flows, destinations, ports, and protocol activity.
Authentication Logs | High | Strong evidence for login activity, failed attempts, access patterns, and privilege use.
SIEM Correlation Alert | Medium | Useful for detection, but should be validated against raw logs or supporting telemetry.
Threat Intelligence Match | Medium | Useful for context, but reputation alone should not confirm compromise.
Analyst Notes | Medium | Useful for interpretation, but should be supported by technical evidence.
User Report | Low to Medium | Useful for context, but requires technical validation.
External Advisory | Medium | Useful for broader threat context, but may not prove local compromise.

6. Technical Findings

6.1 Alert and Telemetry Analysis
From a SOC investigation perspective, the available evidence shows alert first observed by monitoring source, followed by triage completed and severity/confidence assigned, followed by investigation completed or completed with limitations. This activity is consistent with WannaCry ransomware activity, supported by EV-001: severity: Critical. EV-002: risk_score: 98.0. EV-003: host: FINANCE-WKS-017. EV-004: ip: 10.20.14.117. EV-005: username: ACME\\lim.huiwen. MITRE ATT&CK context includes Impact: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact.
The current evidence supports a Critical-severity, High-confidence Likely True Positive, but it does not fully confirm whether payload execution completed, whether credentials or sessions were exposed, or whether follow-on access occurred. The analyst should validate script block logs, process completion status, identity logs, mailbox audit logs, and outbound network telemetry before closure.
Formal chain-of-custody metadata was not supplied in the current source context. Evidence IDs in this report are reporting references only and should be validated against the source case folder or SIEM export before legal or forensic use.

6.2 Affected Assets
Affected assets summary:
- FINANCE-WKS-017: 10.20.14.117, Endpoint
- Evidence gap: No additional host telemetry was supplied.
Hostname | IP Address | Type
FINANCE-WKS-017 | 10.20.14.117 | Endpoint

6.3 Affected Users
Affected users summary:
- ACME\lim.huiwen (values unavailable)
- Evidence gap: No additional host telemetry was supplied.
Username
ACME\lim.huiwen

http://www.iuqerfsodp9ifjaposdfjhgosurijfaewrwergwea.com/ | url | No external reputation supplied | Observed | Reporting Context | EV-014

7. Attack Mapping

7.1 MITRE ATT&CK Mapping
Tactic | Technique | Reason / Evidence | Confidence
Impact | T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact | Recovered from deterministic report context. | High

7.2 Cyber Kill Chain Mapping
Kill Chain Phase | Observed? | Evidence
Reconnaissance | Not Confirmed | No evidence provided unless listed in timeline.
Weaponisation | Not Confirmed | No evidence provided unless listed in timeline.
Delivery | Not Confirmed | No direct evidence provided.
Exploitation | Requires validation | Validate payload execution, suspicious commands, or vulnerable service activity.
Installation | Requires validation | Validate persistence, dropped files, services, scheduled tasks, or registry changes.
Command and Control | Requires validation | Validate network connections, DNS, proxy logs, and threat intelligence matches.
Actions on Objectives | Requires validation | Validate data access, exfiltration, encryption, disruption, or lateral movement.

7.3 Attack Narrative
Based on the available evidence, the likely attack narrative is:
The likely attack sequence began with alert first observed by monitoring source, followed by triage completed and severity/confidence assigned, followed by investigation completed or completed with limitations. This sequence is consistent with WannaCry ransomware activity, with observed evidence including EV-001: severity: Critical. EV-002: risk_score: 98.0. EV-003: host: FINANCE-WKS-017. EV-004: ip: 10.20.14.117. EV-005: username: ACME\\lim.huiwen. The activity has been mapped to Impact: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact.
However, the current evidence does not fully confirm payload completion, persistence, credential or session exposure, lateral movement, data access, or exfiltration. These items should remain open until the SOC analyst validates the relevant endpoint, identity, network, and application logs. Formal chain-of-custody metadata was not supplied in the current source context. Evidence IDs in this report are reporting references only and should be validated against the source case folder or SIEM export before legal or forensic use.

8. Impact Assessment
Business Impact: Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence.
Security Risk: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps.
Not Yet Confirmed: Further compromise, spread, persistence, and data exposure remain subject to analyst validation.
Impact Confidence: High

8.1 Analyst Impact Explanation
Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence. Security risk: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps. Not yet confirmed: Further compromise, spread, persistence, and data exposure remain subject to analyst validation.

8.2 Data Impact Assessment
Data impact assessment: No evidence of data access, data exfiltration, encryption impact, or personal data exposure was provided in the current telemetry. These items remain unresolved and require analyst validation before closure or notification decisions.
- Data access: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Data exfiltration: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Encryption / modification / deletion: Not confirmed by supplied host telemetry
- Personal data involvement: Cannot determine from current evidence
- Notification requirement: Legal / Compliance decision only if data impact is confirmed
- Business impact context: Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence.
- Security risk context: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps.

9. SOC Analyst Review Guidance

9.1 Analyst Review Guidance
The SOC analyst should review this case by validating the evidence that supports the Critical severity, High confidence, and Likely True Positive classification. The most important review areas are the affected scope (FINANCE-WKS-017 (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation criticality, Endpoint)), the affected user context (ACME\lim.huiwen (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation, MFA: Pending analyst validation)), suspicious indicators (No malicious or suspicious IOCs provided), and the timeline sequence (Alert first observed by monitoring source. Triage completed and severity/confidence assigned. Investigation completed or completed with limitations. SOC analyst approval decision recorded. Report generated for SOC analyst review.). The analyst should confirm whether the evidence supports containment, escalation, closure, or further investigation, especially because these validation items remain open: Medium: No additional host telemetry was supplied. If containment is disruptive, the analyst should confirm the approval status (approved) and containment status (not_contained) before action is taken. Formal chain-of-custody metadata was not supplied in the current source context. Evidence IDs in this report are reporting references only and should be validated against the source case folder or SIEM export before legal or forensic use. Any finding that cannot be verified from the provided evidence should remain marked as not confirmed rather than being treated as a confirmed compromise.

9.2 SOC Analyst Review Checklist
1. Confirm that the incident ID, alert ID, severity (Critical), confidence (High), and classification (Likely True Positive) match the latest triage and investigation records. 2. Validate the affected scope by checking whether FINANCE-WKS-017 (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation criticality, Endpoint) and ACME\lim.huiwen (Pending analyst validation, Pending analyst validation, MFA: Pending analyst validation) are present in the original alert, endpoint telemetry, identity logs, or NetWitness evidence. 3. Review the listed IOCs and supporting evidence references, especially No malicious or suspicious IOCs provided, before blocking, isolating, or escalating based on indicator reputation. 4. Reconstruct the timeline using the available events (Alert first observed by monitoring source. Triage completed and severity/confidence assigned. Investigation completed or completed with limitations. SOC analyst approval decision recorded. Report generated for SOC analyst review.) and confirm whether payload execution, persistence, credential exposure, lateral movement, data access, or exfiltration is not confirmed in the provided evidence. 5. Compare the technical evidence against the mapped MITRE ATT&CK context (Impact: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact) and record whether each mapped behaviour is observed, suggested, or still pending validation. 6. Review unresolved evidence gaps (Medium: No additional host telemetry was supplied.) and collect the required endpoint, identity, network, email, or application logs before closure. 7. Decide whether containment or approval should proceed by checking the current approval status (approved) and containment status (not_contained). 8. Record the final analyst decision, including whether the case should be escalated, contained, closed as false positive, or kept open for further investigation.

P2 | Validate whether mssecsvc.exe is present and running on the host. | Endpoint Response Team | No | Based on Critical severity, Likely True Positive classification, FINANCE-WKS-017 scope, and 9 technical indicator(s) linked to evidence (EV-006).

12.3 Control Gap Assessment
Control Domain | Gap Identified | Risk Level
Identity and Access Management | Validate credential exposure, MFA status, suspicious logins, and privileged access. | Critical
Endpoint Security | Validate endpoint process execution, malware artefacts, containment status, and persistence. | Critical
Network Security | Validate suspicious outbound traffic, C2 indicators, DNS, proxy, and firewall activity. | Critical
Logging and Monitoring | Validate whether logs are complete enough to reconstruct the incident. | High
Vulnerability Management | Validate whether exploitation involved known vulnerability or missing patch. | Requires validation
Backup and Recovery | Validate restoration needs if disruption, encryption, or destructive activity is found. | Requires validation
User Awareness | Validate whether user action contributed to the incident, especially for phishing. | Requires validation
Incident Response Process | Validate approval, escalation, evidence preservation, and closure workflow. | Critical

13. Control and Framework Mapping

13.2 Policy Application Summary
Source | Relevant Guidance | Application to This Case
incident_severity_policy.md | Severity should reflect evidence, impact, and confidence. | Current severity is Critical and should be analyst validated.
report_writing_sop.md | Reports must separate evidence-backed facts from gaps. | The export keeps missing facts as evidence gaps rather than inventing details.

13.3 Knowledge Files Used

- procedures/report_writing_sop.md
- procedures/investigation_sop.md

13.4 Playbooks Excluded in This Version
- malware_response_playbook.md
> Raw retrieved RAG snippets are stored in enriched_reporting_context.json for traceability, but this report only shows the analyst-friendly policy application summary.

14. Lessons Learnt

14.1 What Worked Well
Detection: Alert context was available from RSA NetWitness SIEM.
Alert Processing: Reporting Agent consolidated enriched alert, triage, investigation, and approval inputs.
Threat Intelligence: 9 IOC(s) were available for reporting and evidence linking.
Triage: Severity and confidence were normalised, with stale or missing fields flagged.
Reporting: Evidence gaps and validation status were explicitly documented.

14.2 What Did Not Work Well / Requires Validation
Evidence gaps and limitations are listed once in section 6.6. Use that section for analyst validation before closure.

14.3 Opportunities for Improvement
Detection Engineering: Tune detection rules based on confirmed true positive or false positive outcome.
Threat Intelligence: Record IOC source, confidence, reputation, and evidence references.
Playbook Maturity: Ensure the relevant playbook lists required evidence, approval points, and closure criteria.
Automation: Prevent stale context by validating severity, confidence, incident ID, and latest workflow stage before report generation.
Analyst Review: Require human review before closure, disruptive containment, or management reporting.
Evidence Handling: Preserve evidence references and avoid unsupported narrative claims.

15. Recommendations and Remediation Roadmap

15.1 Priority Recommendations
Priority recommendations are listed in section 10.3 Recommended Actions to avoid repeating the same action table without additional value.

15.2 Short-Term Actions, 0 to 30 Days
- Validate missing evidence gaps listed in this report.
- Confirm containment and analyst approval status.
- Review and block confirmed malicious IOCs where appropriate.
- Preserve relevant logs, alerts, and endpoint evidence.

15.3 Medium-Term Actions, 31 to 90 Days
- Improve detection logic based on confirmed findings.
- Update the relevant playbook with any missing investigation pivots.
- Review account, endpoint, email, and network controls related to the incident.
- Conduct a post-incident review with SOC, IT operations, and business stakeholders.

15.4 Long-Term Actions, Beyond 90 Days
- Improve incident response maturity using lessons learnt.
- Build stronger control mapping across detection, response, and recovery.
- Expand automation coverage for evidence collection, validation, and report quality checks.
- Maintain a prior-case knowledge base after SOC analyst approval.

16. Management Response and Action Plan
Management response is required for the recommended actions above. Target dates and owners should be assigned during management review when they are not already present in the source case context.

17. Final Assessment
The report is suitable for SOC analyst review with status Generated for analyst review. Before closure, the analyst should validate unresolved evidence gaps, confirm containment or approval decisions, and determine whether any business, data, regulatory, or recovery impact exists. Any disruptive action, such as endpoint isolation or credential revocation, should follow the recorded approval workflow. Data impact assessment: No evidence of data access, data exfiltration, encryption impact, or personal data exposure was provided in the current telemetry. These items remain unresolved and require analyst validation before closure or notification decisions.
- Data access: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Data exfiltration: No evidence provided, requires validation
- Encryption / modification / deletion: Not confirmed by supplied host telemetry
- Personal data involvement: Cannot determine from current evidence
- Notification requirement: Legal / Compliance decision only if data impact is confirmed
- Business impact context: Current business impact depends on validated affected assets, users, data access, and service disruption evidence.
- Security risk context: Security risk is tied to the WannaCry ransomware activity scenario and should be validated using the listed evidence and gaps.
Incident severity confirmed: Pass
Confidence confirmed: Pass
Root cause assessed: Partial
Containment status recorded: Pass
Recovery validated: Requires analyst review
Business owner informed: Not evidenced in supplied source context
Recommendations documented: Pass
Analyst review completed: Requires final analyst review

Appendix E: Chain of Custody
Formal chain-of-custody metadata was not supplied in the current source context. Evidence IDs in this report are reporting references only and should be validated against the source case folder or SIEM export before legal or forensic use.
